classes.Barbarian: A fierce warrior of primitive background who can enter a battle rage.
classes.Bard: An inspiring magician whose power echoes the music of creation.
classes.Cleric: A priestly champion who wields divine magic in service of a higher power.
classes.Druid: A priest of the Old Faith, wielding the powers of nature and adopting animal forms.
classes.Fighter: A master of martial combat, skilled with a variety of weapons and armor.
classes.Monk: A master of martial arts, harnessing the power of the body in pursuit of physical and spiritual perfection.
classes.Paladin: A holy warrior bound to a sacred oath.
classes.Ranger: A warrior who uses martial prowess and nature magic to combat threats on the edges of civilization.
classes.Rogue: A scoundrel who uses stealth and trickery to overcome obstacles and enemies.
classes.Sorcerer: A spellcaster who draws on inborn magic from a gift or bloodline.
classes.Warlock: A wielder of magic that is derived from a bargain with an extraplanar entity.
classes.Wizard: A scholarly magic-user capable of wielding cosom-altering powers.

alignments.Lawful Good: Typically acts as a good person should be expected to act. They combine a commitment to opposing evil with the discipline to fight relentlessly.
alignments.Neutral Good: Do the best that a good person can do. They are devoted to helping others and work with kings and magistrates but do not feel beholden to them.
alignments.Chaotic Good: Act as their conscience directs, with little regard for what others expect. They make their own way, but they're kind and benevolent.
alignments.Lawful Neutral: Act in accordance with law, tradition, or personal codes. Order and organization are paramount to them.
alignments.True Neutral: The character who prefers to steer clear of moral questions and don't take sides, doing what seems appropriate at the time.
alignments.Chaotic Neutral: Follow their whims, holding their personal freedom above all else. They are individualists first and last.
alignments.Lawful Evil: Methodically take what they want, within the limits of a code of tradition, loyalty, or order.
alignments.Neutral Evil: The alignment of those who do whatever they can get away with, without compassion or qualms.
alignments.Chaotic Evil: Act with arbitrary violence, spurred by their greed, hatred, or bloodlust. Their only desire is for personal gain.

stats.STR: Strength measures physical power and athletic training. It's used for physical force, lifting, and melee attacks.
stats.DEX: Dexterity measures agility, reflexes, and balance. It affects Armor Class and ranged attacks.
stats.CON: Constitution measures health, stamina, and vital force. It determines your Hit Points.
stats.INT: Intelligence measures mental acuity, information recall, and analytical skill. Crucial for wizards.
stats.WIS: Wisdom measures awareness, intuition, and insight. Important for clerics and druids.
stats.CHA: Charisma measures your ability to interact effectively with others and social influence.

skills.Athletics: Covers difficult situations you encounter while climbing, jumping, or swimming.
skills.Acrobatics: Your ability to stay on your feet in a tricky situation, like walking across ice or balancing on a tightrope.
skills.Sleight of Hand: Manual trickery, such as planting something on someone or concealing an object on your person.
skills.Stealth: Escaping notice by moving quietly and hiding from others.
skills.Arcana: Your ability to recall lore about spells, magic items, eldritch symbols, and the planes of existence.
skills.History: Your ability to recall lore about historical events, legendary people, ancient kingdoms, and former cultures.
skills.Investigation: Looking for clues and making deductions based on those clues.
skills.Nature: Your ability to recall lore about terrain, plants and animals, the weather, and natural cycles.
skills.Religion: Your ability to recall lore about deities, rites and prayers, religious hierarchies, and holy symbols.
skills.Animal Handling: Calming down a domesticated animal, keeping a mount from getting spooked, or intuiting an animal’s intentions.
skills.Insight: Determining the true intentions of a creature, such as searching out a lie or predicting someone’s next move.
skills.Medicine: Stabilizing a dying companion or diagnosing an illness.
skills.Perception: Your general awareness of your surroundings and the keenness of your senses.
skills.Survival: Following tracks, hunting wild game, guiding your group through frozen wastelands, and noticing signs that owlbears live nearby.
skills.Deception: Hiding the truth, either verbally or through your actions.
skills.Intimidation: When you attempt to influence someone through overt threats, hostile actions, or physical violence.
skills.Performance: Delighting an audience with music, dance, acting, storytelling, or some other form of entertainment.
skills.Persuasion: Influencing someone with tact, social graces, or good nature.
